Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{6.01}{14.42}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.42}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{6.01}{{901.65} - {8.81} - {79.37}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{6.01}{813.47}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {56}) = {0.42}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {374}  \quad n_{B_1} = {30}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {397}  \quad n_{B_2} = {30}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.01}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{374}^2}{30} + \frac{{397}^2}{30}} - \frac{{771}^2}{60}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{139876}}{30} + \frac{{157609}}{30}} - \frac{{594441}}{60}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{4662.53} + {5253.63}} - {9907.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {9916.16} - {9907.35}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {8.81}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{8.81}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {8.81}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{8.81}{14.42}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.61}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{8.81}{{901.65} - {6.01} - {79.37}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{8.81}{816.27}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {56}) = {0.61}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.01}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {94.19} - {6.01} - {8.81}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {79.37}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{79.37}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {79.37}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{79.37}{14.42}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {5.5}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{79.37}{{901.65} - {6.01} - {8.81}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{79.37}{886.83}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.09}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {56}) = {5.5}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.09}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 16
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {772} \quad M_{A1B1} = {55.14}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {2057.71} \quad n_{A1B1} = {14}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {794} \quad M_{A1B2} = {56.71}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {4234.86} \quad n_{A1B2} = {14}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {900} \quad M_{A2B1} = {64.29}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {640.86} \quad n_{A2B1} = {14}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {724} \quad M_{A2B2} = {51.71}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {2136.86} \quad n_{A2B2} = {14}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {56} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {55}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {56} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {52}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {191970} - \frac{3190^2}{56}\)  \(SS_{total} = {191970} - \frac{10176100}{56}\)  \(SS_{total} = {191970} - {181716.07}\)  \(SS_{total} = {10253.93}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {2057.71 + 4234.86 + 640.86 + 2136.86}\)  \(SS_{within} = {9070.29}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {10253.93} - {9070.29}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1183.64}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {1566}  \quad n_{A_1} = {28}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {1624}  \quad n_{A_2} = {28}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.03}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{1566}^2}{28} + \frac{{1624}^2}{28}} - \frac{{3190}^2}{56}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{2452356}}{28} + \frac{{2637376}}{28}} - \frac{{10176100}}{56}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{87584.14} + {94192.0}} - {181716.07}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {181776.14} - {181716.07}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {60.07}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{60.07}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {60.07}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{60.07}{174.43}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.34}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{60.07}{{10253.93} - {423.51} - {700.06}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{60.07}{9130.36}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {52}) = {0.34}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1672}  \quad n_{B_1} = {28}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1518}  \quad n_{B_2} = {28}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.03}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1672}^2}{28} + \frac{{1518}^2}{28}} - \frac{{3190}^2}{56}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{2795584}}{28} + \frac{{2304324}}{28}} - \frac{{10176100}}{56}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{99842.29} + {82297.29}} - {181716.07}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {182139.58} - {181716.07}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {423.51}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{423.51}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {423.51}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{423.51}{174.43}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {2.43}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{423.51}{{10253.93} - {60.07} - {700.06}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{423.51}{9493.8}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.04}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {52}) = {2.43}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.04}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.03}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1183.64} - {60.07} - {423.51}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {700.06}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{700.06}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {700.06}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{700.06}{174.43}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {4.01}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{700.06}{{10253.93} - {60.07} - {423.51}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{700.06}{9770.35}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.07}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {52}) = {4.01}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.07}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 17
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {366} \quad M_{A1B1} = {45.75}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {1689.5} \quad n_{A1B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {368} \quad M_{A1B2} = {46.0}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {370.0} \quad n_{A1B2} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {462} \quad M_{A2B1} = {57.75}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {997.5} \quad n_{A2B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {446} \quad M_{A2B2} = {55.75}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {1243.5} \quad n_{A2B2} = {8}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {367} \quad M_{A3B1} = {45.88}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {446.88} \quad n_{A3B1} = {8}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {431} \quad M_{A3B2} = {53.88}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {1350.88} \quad n_{A3B2} = {8}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {48} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {47}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {48} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {42}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {131372} - \frac{2440^2}{48}\)  \(SS_{total} = {131372} - \frac{5953600}{48}\)  \(SS_{total} = {131372} - {124033.33}\)  \(SS_{total} = {7338.67}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1689.5 + 370.0 + 997.5 + 1243.5 + 446.88 + 1350.88}\)  \(SS_{within} = {6098.26}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {7338.67} - {6098.26}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1240.41}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {734}  \quad n_{A_1} = {16}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {908}  \quad n_{A_2} = {16}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {798}  \quad n_{A_3} = {16}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.15}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{734}^2}{16} + \frac{{908}^2}{16} + \frac{{798}^2}{16}} - \frac{{2440}^2}{48}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{538756}}{16} + \frac{{824464}}{16} + \frac{{636804}}{16}} - \frac{{5953600}}{48}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{33672.25} + {51529.0} + {39800.25}} - {124033.33}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {125001.5} - {124033.33}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {968.17}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{968.17}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {484.08}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{484.08}{145.2}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {3.33}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{968.17}{{7338.67} - {52.09} - {220.15}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{968.17}{7066.43}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.14}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {42}) = {3.33}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.14}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {1195}  \quad n_{B_1} = {24}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {1245}  \quad n_{B_2} = {24}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.28}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1195}^2}{24} + \frac{{1245}^2}{24}} - \frac{{2440}^2}{48}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{1428025}}{24} + \frac{{1550025}}{24}} - \frac{{5953600}}{48}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{59501.04} + {64584.38}} - {124033.33}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {124085.42} - {124033.33}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {52.09}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{52.09}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {52.09}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{52.09}{145.2}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.36}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{52.09}{{7338.67} - {968.17} - {220.15}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{52.09}{6150.35}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {42}) = {0.36}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {5.15}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1240.41} - {968.17} - {52.09}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {220.15}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{220.15}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {110.08}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{110.08}{145.2}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {0.76}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{220.15}{{7338.67} - {968.17} - {52.09}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{220.15}{6318.41}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.03}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {42}) = {0.76}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.03}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 18

Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {97969} - \frac{1661^2}{30}\)  \(SS_{total} = {97969} - \frac{2758921}{30}\)  \(SS_{total} = {97969} - {91964.03}\)  \(SS_{total} = {6004.97}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {614.8 + 778.8 + 1277.2 + 298.8 + 763.2 + 158.8}\)  \(SS_{within} = {3891.6}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {6004.97} - {3891.6}\)  \(SS_{between} = {2113.37}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)

Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{87.06}{154.57}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {0.56}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.16}{{8500.83} - {174.11} - {907.0}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{0.16}{7419.72}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.0}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {0.0}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\)

 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.14}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {337.45} - {178.1} - {55.13}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {104.22}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{104.22}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {52.11}\)

 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.63}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {3052.84} - {81.25} - {2051.51}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {920.08}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{920.08}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {230.02}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{920.08}{{7238.44} - {81.25} - {2051.51}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{920.08}{5105.68}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.18}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({4}, {36}) = {1.98}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.18}\)

 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1742.64}{{26199.3} - {915.22} - {3912.93}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1742.64}{21371.15}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.08}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {126}) = {5.59}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.08}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {2399}  \quad n_{B_1} = {45}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {2543}  \quad n_{B_2} = {45}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {2256}  \quad n_{B_3} = {45}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.07}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{2399}^2}{45} + \frac{{2543}^2}{45} + \frac{{2256}^2}{45}} - \frac{{7198}^2}{135}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{5755201}}{45} + \frac{{6466849}}{45} + \frac{{5089536}}{45}} - \frac{{51811204}}{135}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{127893.36} + {143707.76} + {113100.8}} - {383786.7}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {384701.92} - {383786.7}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {915.22}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{915.22}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {457.61}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{457.61}{155.78}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {2.94}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{915.22}{{26199.3} - {1742.64} - {3912.93}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{915.22}{20543.73}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.04}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {126}) = {2.94}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.04}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {4}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.44}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {6570.79} - {1742.64} - {915.22}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {3912.93}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{3912.93}{4}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {978.23}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{978.23}{155.78}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {6.28}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3912.93}{{26199.3} - {1742.64} - {915.22}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{3912.93}{23541.44}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.17}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {126}) = {6.28}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.17}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 29
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {387} \quad M_{A1B1} = {29.77}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {652.31} \quad n_{A1B1} = {13}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {507} \quad M_{A1B2} = {39.0}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {240.0} \quad n_{A1B2} = {13}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {547} \quad M_{A2B1} = {42.08}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {630.92} \quad n_{A2B1} = {13}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {405} \quad M_{A2B2} = {31.15}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {239.69} \quad n_{A2B2} = {13}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {52} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {51}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {4} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {3}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {52} - {4}\)  \(df_{within} = {48}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {68690} - \frac{1846^2}{52}\)  \(SS_{total} = {68690} - \frac{3407716}{52}\)  \(SS_{total} = {68690} - {65533.0}\)  \(SS_{total} = {3157.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {652.31 + 240.0 + 630.92 + 239.69}\)  \(SS_{within} = {1762.92}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {3157.0} - {1762.92}\)  \(SS_{between} = {1394.08}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {894}  \quad n_{A_1} = {26}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {952}  \quad n_{A_2} = {26}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.04}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{894}^2}{26} + \frac{{952}^2}{26}} - \frac{{1846}^2}{52}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{799236}}{26} + \frac{{906304}}{26}} - \frac{{3407716}}{52}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{30739.85} + {34857.85}} - {65533.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {65597.7} - {65533.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {64.7}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{64.7}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {64.7}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{64.7}{36.73}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {1.76}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{64.7}{{3157.0} - {9.3} - {1320.08}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{64.7}{1827.62}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.04}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {1.76}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.04}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {934}  \quad n_{B_1} = {26}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {912}  \quad n_{B_2} = {26}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.04}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{934}^2}{26} + \frac{{912}^2}{26}} - \frac{{1846}^2}{52}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{872356}}{26} + \frac{{831744}}{26}} - \frac{{3407716}}{52}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{33552.15} + {31990.15}} - {65533.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {65542.3} - {65533.0}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {9.3}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{9.3}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {9.3}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{9.3}{36.73}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {0.25}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{9.3}{{3157.0} - {64.7} - {1320.08}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{9.3}{1772.22}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.01}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {0.25}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.01}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {3} - {1} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {1}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.04}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {1394.08} - {64.7} - {9.3}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {1320.08}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{1320.08}{1}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {1320.08}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{1320.08}{36.73}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {35.94}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1320.08}{{3157.0} - {64.7} - {9.3}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{1320.08}{3083.0}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.43}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {48}) = {35.94}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.43}\)

title: Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
Problem 30
Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {193} \quad M_{A1B1} = {17.55}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {40.73} \quad n_{A1B1} = {11}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {148} \quad M_{A1B2} = {13.45}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {10.73} \quad n_{A1B2} = {11}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {183} \quad M_{A2B1} = {16.64}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {70.55} \quad n_{A2B1} = {11}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {194} \quad M_{A2B2} = {17.64}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {36.55} \quad n_{A2B2} = {11}\)  \(T_{A3B1} = {167} \quad M_{A3B1} = {15.18}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {43.64} \quad n_{A3B1} = {11}\)  \(T_{A3B2} = {119} \quad M_{A3B2} = {10.82}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {55.64} \quad n_{A3B2} = {11}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {66} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {65}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {66} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {60}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {15924} - \frac{1004^2}{66}\)  \(SS_{total} = {15924} - \frac{1008016}{66}\)  \(SS_{total} = {15924} - {15272.97}\)  \(SS_{total} = {651.03}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {40.73 + 10.73 + 70.55 + 36.55 + 43.64 + 55.64}\)  \(SS_{within} = {257.84}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {651.03} - {257.84}\)  \(SS_{between} = {393.19}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {341}  \quad n_{A_1} = {22}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {377}  \quad n_{A_2} = {22}\)  \(T_{A_3} = {286}  \quad n_{A_3} = {22}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.98}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{341}^2}{22} + \frac{{377}^2}{22} + \frac{{286}^2}{22}} - \frac{{1004}^2}{66}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{116281}}{22} + \frac{{142129}}{22} + \frac{{81796}}{22}} - \frac{{1008016}}{66}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{5285.5} + {6460.41} + {3718.0}} - {15272.97}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {15463.91} - {15272.97}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {190.94}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{190.94}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {95.47}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{95.47}{4.3}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {22.2}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{190.94}{{651.03} - {101.88} - {100.37}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{190.94}{448.78}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.43}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {60}) = {22.2}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.43}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {543}  \quad n_{B_1} = {33}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {461}  \quad n_{B_2} = {33}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {7.08}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{543}^2}{33} + \frac{{461}^2}{33}} - \frac{{1004}^2}{66}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{294849}}{33} + \frac{{212521}}{33}} - \frac{{1008016}}{66}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{8934.82} + {6440.03}} - {15272.97}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {15374.85} - {15272.97}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {101.88}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{101.88}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {101.88}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{101.88}{4.3}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {23.69}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{101.88}{{651.03} - {190.94} - {100.37}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{101.88}{359.72}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.28}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({1}, {60}) = {23.69}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.28}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {2} - {1}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.98}, \alpha = {0.01}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {393.19} - {190.94} - {101.88}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {100.37}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{100.37}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {50.18}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{50.18}{4.3}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {11.67}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{100.37}{{651.03} - {190.94} - {101.88}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{100.37}{358.21}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.28}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {60}) = {11.67}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.28}\)
